task: I need to send a short update to a few people about a work issue that’s still in progress, and I want to make sure I cover the key points without leaving anything important out. Can you help me draft a message that feels professional and easy to follow?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a clean version:

> Hi everyone — quick update on this issue. We’ve confirmed [what’s confirmed], and we’re still checking [what’s still open]. That’s the piece holding it up right now. I’ll send the next update by [day/time].

If you want it a little warmer:

> Hi everyone — quick update: we’ve confirmed [what’s confirmed], and we’re still verifying [what’s still open]. Once that’s done, I’ll share the next step and timing by [day/time].

If you want, I can also make it:
- more direct
- more manager-style
- or shorter for Slack/text

user_simulator.text:
Close, but I’d want it a little more specific and less generic. The brackets are fine as a template, but I need something I can actually send without sounding vague. Also, if this is going to a few people, I’d want it to cover: what’s been confirmed, what’s still being reviewed, whether anything changed, and when they should expect the next update. Short is good. Just not fluffy.

Can you rewrite it with those points built in — still professional, still easy to read?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter version that hits those points without sounding fluffy:

> Hi everyone — quick update: we’ve confirmed [what’s confirmed], and we’re still reviewing [what’s still being checked]. Nothing has changed on the confirmed part, and the remaining piece is what’s holding the update up right now. I’ll send the next update by [day/time].

If you want it a touch more natural:

> Hi everyone — quick update: we’ve confirmed [what’s confirmed], and we’re still reviewing [what’s still being checked]. The confirmed part hasn’t changed; we’re just waiting on the remaining piece before I can close this out. Next update by [day/time].

If you want, I can also make it:
- **more direct**
- **more manager-style**
- or **short enough for Slack**

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Clean and not overdone. I can use that.
